about:
There's a $50 trillion savings gap in this country. Most of it lives in the space between people who need it most and systems that were never built to serve them — businesses without 401(k)s families without healthcare savings individuals with disabilities or those saving for higher education while navigating a financial world that wasn't designed for them.
 
Vestwell is the infrastructure that changes that. We're the cloud-native engine powering retirement healthcare education disability and child savings programs for financial institutions payroll companies and government agencies who want to reach everyday savers but need the technology to do it right. Today we power over 500K+ businesses 2M savers and $50B in assets.
 
We're not a benefits company. We're the engine underneath the savings economy powering 401(k) 403(b) 457 IRA 529 ABLE emergency savings child savings and student debt paydown savings programs on one AI-driven cloud-native proprietary technology.
 
Our obsession is driven by how many people finally have access to a savings account they didn't have before.
 
Founder Chairman & CEO. EY Entrepreneur of the Year New York 2024. InvestmentNews 40 Under 40. Duke Fuqua MBA. Illinois Finance.

current_position_description:
Healiom: the cursor for agent-native care.

Most software still assumes people do the work agents change that—and healthcare is where it matters most.

SMB care orgs deliver most U.S. care yet run underwater: too few clinicians too many clicks and a $350 B capacity and quality gap. Bolting AI onto form-based EHRs is like putting wings on a car—it never flies. In contrast Agent-native systems are intent-based reasoning and acting rather than recording and routing.

Healiom is building the Autonomous Health Record (AHR)—new infrastructure for an agent-native world where services and ops are delivered by agents and directed by humans. One system one experience three layers: Symbiotic Intelligence with a Large Medical Model Specialized Agents for providers and patients and a collaborative workspace that closes the loop end-to-end.

The system listens sees and acts across front middle back and extended office to turn intent into completed care—driving real-time differential diagnoses guideline-aligned orders quality-anchored documentation and RCM that just works.

We've built hard things before. The system was software the system will be an agent. In care that means an Institutional AI Physician Agent.

Healiom: care ops by agents directed by humans.
company_description:
At Healiom we're delivering technology that works for providers not against them. Our AI-elevated platform connects disparate systems seamlessly removing friction from patient care workflows. The result? Clinicians in emergency departments urgent care centers and ambulatory practices can focus on what matters most: caring for patients and reclaiming their lives.

Built by physicians for physicians Healiom was designed with a deep understanding of what's at stake—for patients and providers alike. Our intuitive platform reduces clicks simplifies processes and ensures a quick implementation timeline from signature to go-live. With 24/7 access to emergency medicine specialists we support patient care in even the most challenging environments from single-provider EDs to high-volume urgent care centers and ambulatory practices.

Healiom Delivers Results That Matter:
•Improved Provider Satisfaction: Get out within 5 minutes of your shift's end.
•Enhanced Patient Outcomes: Real-time specialist access and QA elevate care standards.
•Streamlined Operations: Frictionless workflows faster implementation and better results.

Whether managing an ED stretched thin balancing throughput and quality in urgent care or striving for operational excellence in private practice Healiom makes care seamless.

Why Healiom?
Healthcare technology should enhance lives—not complicate them. Everything we do is built to ensure positive care experiences for everyone involved. Together we're creating a future where providers thrive patients heal and care systems work better for all.

Because at Healiom Better is Never Enough.

#Healiom #HealthcareInnovation #WorkflowEfficiency #PatientCenteredCare #AIInHealthcare #DisruptionForGood #BetterIsNeverEnough

company_description:
Wag! Group Co. is the developer of Wag! the on-demand dog walking platform.

At Wag! we're crazy about dogs and the people who love them and everything we do is intended to bring them joy and keep them safe. Our company was founded in 2015 and born from a love of dogs and an entrepreneurial spirit with the aim to make pet parenthood just a little bit easier so dogs and their humans can share a fulfilling life full of joyful moments.

We invented on-demand dog walking by connecting an already passionate community of local dog walkers with Pet Parents. Launching in Los Angeles Wag! services are now available in more than 5 300 cities nationwide. The 500 000 Pet Caregivers using Wag! are thoroughly vetted and pass a rigorous screening process and our services are bonded and insured.

Wag! donates proceeds from walks to the Greater Good Charities GOODS program and has already provided more than 16 million meals to shelter dogs in the United States.

about:
Core Diagnostic Corp. 
Nasopharyngeal Aspirate Collection Device “NACD”. Non Provisional U.S. Patent: #17/368 680

Mission:
"To Provide Innovative affordable infectious disease diagnostic products that empower your healthcare decisions”
Problem:
The amount of live virus in a small-volume nasal swab specimen may be critically less than that in a sample obtained by aspiration. For this reason Nasopharyngeal aspirates or nasal wash specimens are generally considered the specimens of choice for the detection of respiratory viruses. Unfortunately obtaining an aspirate using conventional nasal aspirators can be cumbersome and it requires a specialized device such as an electric suction/vacuum device. 
These features make it unfeasible for widespread use in clinical practice not to mention unpleasant for the patient. 

Solution:
CoreDx Product development team has designed a single-use and easy to use nasal wash aspirator. In this design a fixed amount of sterile fluid is gently sprayed through an innovative dispensing nozzle into a patient’s nasal passage and then aspirated back into the vial. The vial with sterile fluid and nozzle are fully integrated into one unit so the wash and aspiration can be done in one single step. No separate vacuum device is necessary making it feasible to use by clinicians and patients. The number one reason for false negative test "not enough sample size to evaluate" the NACD will provide the lab tech with enough of the patients sample to eliminate a false negative results. 

 https://youtu.be/Oik3857_-YI

Infectious Disease ~ The Greatest Enemy of All!

Biosortia is pursuing multiple discoveries to IND opportunities with microbiome focussed immuno-oncology and immunological (IO/I) discoveries with a focus of early licensing and royalties in a massive multi-billion dollar market. Biosortia is the only company obtaining the deep dark chemistry from natural aquatic microbiomes which are the most sought after but previously unobtainable drug like chemistry. Typical research efforts could only get at the 1% of culturable microorganisms and the chemicals produced during culture has led to more than 25% of the drugs on the market. The other 99% of unculturable microorganisms and associated chemistry has not been obtained. Biosortia collects and immediately preserves a significant sample of a chosen microbiome. This includes both the culturable and unculturable to explore the new chemistry produced when single-cell organisms interact and are highly relevant to multiple human therapeutic areas. This research area has proven relevance to human disease with an overlap of greater than 73% to the gut microbiome.

Biosortia is actively seeking supporters and collaborators for its various efforts.

Biosortia believes we can improve the quality of life for individuals and communities by finding and purposing compounds from mostly unculturable microorganisms consortia in the natural competitive environment. This is achieved by utilization of our teams of researchers and partners who discover obtain identify test and study the new compounds for new drugs and other useful chemical compounds in various fields including nutrition agriculture and biotechnology processing.

company_description:
Biosortia Pharmaceuticals is pioneering a revolutionary approach in drug discovery through its Industrial Scale Microbiome Mining (ISMM) technology. By accessing the vast untapped chemical diversity found within natural microbiomes We unlock a hidden world of small molecules that play crucial roles in cellular signaling and disease processes. These small molecules often referred to as the "drug-like dark matter" of cellular biology have been largely inaccessible until now. Our technology allows for the large-scale harvesting and exploration of these compounds providing an unprecedented opportunity to discover novel therapeutic agents.

Through deep and direct access to this hidden chemistry We create extensive data sets that are uniquely positioned to be leveraged by advanced computational and generative AI tools. These tools enable the prioritization of potential drug candidates by predicting their interaction with specific disease targets. By integrating AI with its vast repository of natural products We can perform in silico testing to identify promising molecules which can then be further optimized or derivatized to enhance their efficacy and safety profiles.

Our approach accelerates the drug discovery process by moving seamlessly from computational predictions to real-world application. Our ability to obtain these prioritized molecules directly from their microbiome sources or synthesize derivatives as needed significantly shortens the timeline to preclinical testing and Investigational New Drug (IND) submissions. This capability not only streamlines the development of novel therapeutics but also provides a robust pipeline of next-generation products for the biotech industry.

By combining its proprietary ISMM technology with cutting-edge AI Biosortia is poised to transform the landscape of drug discovery. The company's platform offers biotech firms the ability to build pipeline opportunities rapidly.

We help to bring data-driven products to market - whether via spinouts from the Guild itself by working in the trenches alongside portfolio company teams or in concert with strategic partners. We work in healthcare life sciences renewable energy and climate change – complex sectors whose needs are not well-served by the existing venture ecosystem and in which finding innovative solutions and bringing them to market is crucial for the health and sustainability of our planet and society.

We are steeped in machine learning data science and artificial intelligence but we are adamant that technology choices be driven by a deep understanding of the problem at hand. In order to deliver real-world impact - not just tech showcases - we rely on the following principles in our approach to new product and business development.

The Data Guild is dedicated to building products services and companies that improve the quality of human life and address humanity's greatest challenges.

about:
Diane Hessan is an award-winning entrepreneur and innovator in the market research field recognized nationally for her expertise on the American voter. Since 2016 Diane has been deeply engaged in a longitudinal study of the electorate uncovering trends shifts and common ground among voters. Her insights have been widely shared through over fifty columns in the Boston Globe and featured on CNN NPR The Wall Street Journal The New York Times Forbes Fortune and other major outlets. Her latest book Our Common Ground: Insights from Four Years of Listening to American Voters was published in 2021 by RealClear Publishing.

Diane is the founder and Chairman of C Space formerly known as Communispace the pioneering company that first utilized social media to help major brands gain consumer insights and inspiration. During her fourteen-year tenure as CEO C Space experienced exponential growth collaborating with hundreds of global corporations across twelve countries. Her innovative approach has earned her recognition as a disruptor in the market research industry particularly for groundbreaking research conducted via the internet.

In addition to her work with C Space Diane recently launched Salient Ventures a company that consolidates her investment and advisory efforts to accelerate the next generation of tech startups.

Diane serves on the boards of Panera Eastern Bank Brightcove Sago Mass Challenge Tufts University and Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center. Her contributions have been recognized with numerous awards including the Pinnacle Award from the Greater Boston Chamber of Commerce the Most Admired CEO award the Boston Power 50 from the Boston Business Journal Ernst & Young’s Entrepreneur of the Year the Northstar Award from Springboard and the Greater Boston Chamber of Commerce Entrepreneur of the Year. Additionally she has been inducted into the Babson Academy of Distinguished Entrepreneurs received the Asper Award for Global Entrepreneurship from Brandeis University was elected to NACD's Directorship 100 in 2021 and inducted into the Market Research Hall of Fame in 2023.

Diane coauthored the Business Week best seller Customer-Centered Growth: Five Strategies for Building Competitive Advantage published in eleven languages. She holds a BA in Economics and English from Tufts University and an MBA from Harvard Business School. She has also been awarded honorary degrees from Bentley University and the New England College of Business.

company_description:
INERGIO is a Swiss cleantech company providing clean reliable and cost-efficient power for critical off-grid operations. Our compact fuel cell systems are designed to operate in remote and challenging environments where autonomy low maintenance and consistent performance are essential.

Built on proprietary Solid Oxide Fuel Cell (SOFC) technology INERGIO's systems deliver silent operation zero toxic emissions and long-lasting autonomy. Our flagship fuel cell runs on standard propane a non-toxic affordable widely available fuel. The system can be adapted to hydrogen and other fuels making it a flexible and future-ready solution.

Engineered for reliability in harsh conditions INERGIO's technology is designed to meet the demanding needs of applications such as mobile surveillance (CCTV) oil and gas infrastructure and off-grid traffic management. Our fuel cell systems offer a dependable and autonomous power source where stability and performance are critical.

Founded in 2020 as a spin-off from EPFL and HEIG-VD INERGIO is scaling up production in Renens Switzerland to meet growing global demand.

Visit inergio.com to learn more or contact us at info@inergio.com
headline: Scaling off-grid power solutions @INERGIO Ex-VC turned operator

about:
I lead Buffalo Growth Partners as CEO — private equity reimagined through operators technicians and family-first leadership.

For over a decade I’ve built and scaled industrial service businesses — founding Better Fire Protection growing it to 50+ technicians across 3 years and ultimately exiting to private equity. I then spent 5 years as VP managing 12+ locations 750+ employees and executing a buy-and-build strategy focused on operational excellence integration and disciplined growth.

At BGP we invest where we have unfair advantages: service trades such as inspection testing maintenance HVACR roofing water & septic systems — areas where proven systems strong culture and hands-on leadership make all the difference. 

I believe in working with dirty hands — in businesses built on grit loyalty and purpose. If you’re an owner or operator of a service-based business who values a partner that works beside you drives accountability and unlocks growth let’s connect.

Outside of work I’m a husband father outdoorsman and proud patriot committed to building the foundation of our country one technician-powered business at a time.
company_description:
Private Equity Reimagined: Operator Led. Technician Powered. Family Focused.

Buffalo Growth Partners (BGP) is a private equity firm built by operators for operators. We’re reimagining private equity with a gritty hands-on approach that reflects the values we live by: American dynamism blue-collar grit and family-first leadership.

We operate where we have unfair advantages — sectors where our team has proven skills in the trenches of operations. We invest in industrial service businesses where inspection testing and maintenance are critical to long-term value creation — industries like generators gantry cranes HVACR roofing water wells septic and other similar service trades. In each sector we build focused buy-and-build platforms designed to professionalize operations implement proven systems and processes and scale businesses into enduring market leaders.

BGP is backed by DRO a family office based in Austin Texas that has committed significant capital alongside our LPs. This partnership provides the stability and long-term horizon to create meaningful growth opportunities for both operators and investors.

If you’re an owner/operator who wants a partner that understands service-based businesses — one that rolls up its sleeves works beside you in the trenches and brings the playbook to unlock growth — Buffalo Growth Partners was built with you in mind. Together we can build durable technician-powered companies that strengthen the backbone of American industry.

about:
Founder of XEIA Venture Partners. 

XEIA invests in early stage startups driving Human 3: the era of exponential human flourishing. 

While modern medicine (Human 2) benefited society in incalculable ways it ultimately fell short of its promise because the available tools forced it to operate at an incremental pace with long feedback loops.

Today exponential technologies like AI - which can parse through terabytes of scientific literature and data in seconds - and brain-computer-interfaces biosensors and CRISPR - which enable real-time read-write access to our biology - promise to rapidly accelerate the scientific method flywheel and lead to an exponential explosion in health wellness and performance. 

Human 3 will elevate every area of health and wellbeing but will have the biggest impact in areas where modern medicine has made the least progress: the mental health crisis neurodegeneration chronic disease and aging. These are the “focal points” of our investment strategy. 

Human 3 is not a “sector” - our companies combine life sciences AI hardware software deep tech & health tech in novel ways that often defy conventional categorization. Human 3 is a philosophy of vitality. It is a knowing that the “user experience” of the human body and mind is long overdue for an upgrade. It is the willingness to build and invest in that future. This is Human 3.

company_description:
Humacyte Inc. (Nasdaq: HUMA) is proud to announce that in Dec 2024 FDA granted full approval for our Acellular Tissue Engineered Vessel (ATEV) a first-in-class universally implantable bioengineered human vessel for extremity arterial trauma. This approval is a significant milestone in regenerative medicine offering a new treatment option for patients with severe arterial injuries.See the Prescribing Information including Boxed Warning: https://bit.ly/4dl5tuL 

This is the first approval in our bioengineered ATEV Platform where we are continuing to investigate use of our lab-grown tissues and organs to improve the lives of patients and transform the practice of medicine. We develop and manufacture acellular tissues that are meant to be universally implantable and remodel with the patient's own cells over time. The goal is to grow spare parts for people. Humacyte's ATEV is in clinical trials for arteriovenous access for hemodialysis and peripheral arterial disease. Investigational preclinical development is also underway in coronary artery bypass grafts pediatric heart surgery treatment of type 1 diabetes and multiple novel cell and tissue applications. Humacyte's ATEV for performing hemodialysis in AV Access was the first indication to receive the FDA's Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) expedited review designation and the ATEV technology received priority designation for the treatment of extremity arterial repair by the U.S. Secretary of Defense prior to its FDA approval in Dec 2024 for that purpose. For more information visit https://Humacyte.com.

about:
I'm on a mission to get as much money in the hands of women as possible. 

Together with my husband I built one of the world's first social media agencies. Years later we sold that agency for 8-figures.

In that process I suspected the experience for me as a female founder was different from that of my male peers.

I got to work researching and discovered that there is a big fat exit gap.

Women are capturing 0.8% of exit value in this country.

I'm here to change that.

I've launched The Whisper Group which stands to be the #1 exit-readiness advisory firm dedicated exclusively to women-owned businesses.

My podcast The Exit Whisperer interviews female founders who have exited.

And coming soon The Whisper Collective will be the only community comprised exclusively of exited female founders. 

My job will be done when we close the exit gap.

Until then I'll continue writing speaking and helping female founders every step of the way.
company_description:
The Whisper Group is an exit readiness practice for women owned businesses. We help women from scale through sale and we focus specifically on helping turn their life's work into life-changing assets. We're also home to the largest online collective of exited female founders a powerful private network built for what comes after the deal.

The Whisper Group was co-founded by Carrie Kerpen and Maggie Lord but it is about way more than just their own exit advisory practice. Clients who work with the Whisper Group are paired with exited female founders who have built and sold a business in their specific industry. Clients are led through a process known as the Whisper Way-- a methodology and planning program designed to make a business more valuable at exit. When a client is done with the program they'll have a healthier more valuable business that they can either decide to keep running or join the ranks of women that successfully sold their businesses.

The name "Whisper" comes from the seven tenets a business-owner needs to make their business sellable for maximum value: evaluating their Why How Income Secret sauce Profitability Executive team and Roar factor (i.e. the confidence to sit in a room full of men to negotiate the biggest deal of their lifetime).

But it also comes from the fact that no one is speaking loudly about the exits of women business owners. We talk about fundraising. We talk about bootstrapping. We talk about work-life balance. But we don't often discuss selling for life changing sums of money. And I think that's because we simply don't realize it's possible.

The Whisper Group is here to change that.
headline: Turned a lifestyle business into a life-changing asset- now helping other female founders do the same. Founder of The Whisper Gr

company_description:
LASP Capital invests in publicly-traded equities using a long-only strategy with a focus on global growth companies. LASP looks to invest in companies that have a consistent trajectory of 20-30+% growth targeting large addressable markets using a recurring or highly re-occurring revenue model.

Other financial characteristics that are important to LASP include: high Gross Margins strong returns on Sales & Marketing spend negative Net Working Capital low Capital Expenditure requirements and clear evidence of operating leverage over time (even if profitability / cash flow is still negative currently).

LASP utilizes a propriety valuation methodology to manage the inherent risk of multiple compression that comes with investing in high-quality growth companies.

about:
I’ve always loved to race. First swimming then bikes then cars – if it can go fast then I want to make it go faster.

Understanding who I am in racing is central to understanding who I am in business. For me the pinnacle is cars - I've always been infatuated with them - the power the speed the design. My mom was a chemist for Porsche many years ago so in my case I think it might be genetic! After I sold my first company I started buying cars and then I started racing cars. Being competitive on the track is nearly identical with the things that light me up about building a great business. It probably also explains why I obsess about both.

Here is why I think these similarities are incredibly important in business:

The will to win is a HUGE differentiator. A strong will to sell and deliver value outclasses a marginally (or even materially) superior offering 80% of the time. That same mentality all but guarantees that becoming "best-in-class" is only a matter of time.

A great race strategy is VITAL to success and should be given lots of attention before you get to the track. Once the flag drops speed of thought creativity action and reaction in running the race will lead you to the win. Every meeting every product every company journey is exactly the same.

Business and racing are team sports. If you are going to be competitive you MUST have an effective team behind you. Yes driving the car or leading the company is fun and all but having experts in the critical disciplines and letting them do their job is crucial. Trust = Wins.

Finally knowing the track is key. Listening to people who have run the track know the nuances and can help you navigate and make decisions during the fight is an irreplaceable asset. In building a new company reaction and execution times are everything. 

The competition of business and of racing are central to who I am. Having a wall full of trophies and plaques is a great thing. And the truth is that the REAL reason to go racing or to build a great business is the race itself! These elements are what I bring to each and every company and team that I work with and driving the business outcomes and the culture are why I get up in the morning.

about:
I love computer graphics.

I started working in graphics as a hobby as a high school kid. I was fortunate to be spotted by Samuli Syvähuoko and Markus Mäki founders of Remedy Entertainment and was consequently involved in the development of major computer games 1996-2006. At Remedy I worked on graphics technology --- lighting simulation modeling rendering --- for the worldwide blockbuster hits Max Payne (2001) Max Payne 2 (2003) and Alan Wake (2010). I was also lead programmer on the 3D graphics benchmark Final Reality (1997) which subsequently led to the founding of Futuremark Corporation makers of e.g. the 3DMark series of benchmarks.

Concurrently to my work in games I began PhD studies at Helsinki University of Technology (now Aalto University) together with my colleagues Timo Aila Samuli Laine and Janne Kontkanen. I obtained my doctorate in September 2007.

After graduation I joined the MIT graphics group as a Postdoctoral Associate. At MIT I worked with Frédo Durand and lots of other wonderful people on rendering modeling and hardware architectures. I also taught MIT EECS 6.837 (Introduction to Computer Graphics) in fall 2009.

I joined NVIDIA Research in their Helsinki Finland office in June 2010. I collaborate with Timo Aila Samuli Laine Tero Karras and our overseas members on exciting things related to the future graphics hardware and algorithms.

Finally I'm excited to have joined the faculty at Aalto University (former Helsinki University of Technology) as an assistant professor in September 2012. I'm the first Finnish professor to specialize in graphics and I'm thrilled to get to coach our brightest CS students be their goal either the industry or academia. I split my time with NVIDIA Research.

I am honored to serve on the editorial board of ACM Transactions on Graphics the premier journal for disseminating scholarly work on graphics and Journal for Computer Graphics Techniques an innovative short-turnaround open online journal.
company_description:
We excel and make breakthroughs in and across science art technology and business. We renew our society with research-based knowledge creativity and entrepreneurial mindset. Aalto University has been ranked the 7th best young university in the world (Top 50 under 50 QS 2018) and one of the world's top technology challenger universities (THE 2017) thinking outside the box on research collaboration funding and innovation.

Aalto has six schools with 20 000 students and 5 000 employees of which close to 450 are professors. Our campus is located in Espoo Finland. With over 50% of our academic faculty coming from outside Finland we are a highly international community with strong academic standing. 

At Aalto high-quality research art education and entrepreneurship are promoted hand in hand. Disciplinary excellence is combined with multidisciplinary activities engaging both students and the local innovation ecosystem. Our main campus is quickly transforming into an open collaboration hub that encourages encounters between students researchers industry startups and other partners. With an exceptionally strong entrepreneurial community 70-100 new companies are born in our ecosystem every year. 

Aalto University was founded in 2010 as three leading Finnish universities Helsinki University of Technology the Helsinki School of Economics and the University of Art and Design Helsinki were merged to strengthen Finland's innovative capability.
headline: Professor & Distinguished Research Scientist in Graphics Vision ML

about:
I've spent most of my career as a wartime CEO — the kind of role where runway is limited headwinds are real and every decision about time and money has to count.
 
I've done it three times. At Edmodo EatWith and Plum District I walked into companies that had lost product-market fit rebuilt the business model and created a viable path forward.
 
What that experience gives me in a boardroom is something I don't see often enough: the ability to bridge the gap between what the numbers say should happen and what it actually takes to make it happen. It's easy to say "cut costs by Y and grow revenue by X." What that leaves out is everything — how you sequence it how you protect what's working while you fix what isn't. I've been on the receiving end of that advice. I know what lands and what doesn't.
 
I've walked many miles in the CEO's shoes.
 
If you're looking for a board member who has been in the chair during the hard moments — at a public company navigating a strategic inflection point or a growth-stage company that needs a battle-tested operator — I'd welcome the conversation.

* Seeking board and advisory roles where operator experience in turnaround and business model transformation adds value.

The thruline in my career is helping people get the reliable information they need in order to build the lives they want. My superpower is solving hard problems preferably at scale.

company_description:
Adela is developing best-in-class technology to accelerate the diagnosis and improve the management of cancer through blood tests for minimal residual disease (MRD) monitoring and multi-cancer early detection (MCED). Adela's approach efficiently captures extensive biologically-relevant genomic information from the methylome providing greater opportunity to detect cancer signals in the blood compared to platforms that target a smaller set of genomic regions.

Adela's blood-based tissue-free product ensures universal accessibility to MRD testing for patients with cancer eliminating any dependency on tumor tissue availability. Our test is clinically validated for two applications: predicting and surveilling for recurrence in patients with head & neck cancer (published in Annals of Oncology) and immunotherapy response monitoring in patients with advanced cancer (published in npj Precision Oncology).

about:
Europe needed a proper home for corporate venturing.
So we built one.
I'm Co-founder of EUVC Corporate — where the continent's leading CVC heads Chief Innovation Officers and corporate builders come together to learn connect and push European corporate venturing forward.

What we do:
Invest — Corporate venture capital. Helping corporates get direct investing right.
Partner — Corporate Clienting - Corporate-startup collaboration without equity. Making the relationship work on both sides.
Build — Corporate incubation. Helping large organisations build new companies from within.

We bring this to life through the EUVC Podcast (100+ episodes) closed-door summits and gatherings and our EUVC Academy — practitioner-led masterclasses and AMAs with the people actually doing the work.

One of our flagship programs is the Venture Boards Masterclass — a 1.5-day intensive for corporate investors serving on startup boards. Built with leading European law firms. Capped at 20 executives. Through theory. Real boardroom scenarios board simulations and a private dinner with senior venture investors sharing their war-stories.

Before this: Partner at Maersk Growth where I invested $80M+ across the portfolio — including early bets on Einride and Forto. And CFO for Heartcore Capital for a decade.

I have covered the full agricultural value chain including food and beverages alcoholic beverages and spirits fashion and luxury goods consumer electronics and consumer health (OTC devices and pharma). Disruption and change often driven by technology or digital advancements has been a recurring theme across my career. I also have extensive management experience in the professional services space having led or been on the leadership teams of several multi-billion-dollar P&Ls often leading during a period of change or disruption (for example integrating Booz with PWC consulting or DV and BCGs consulting business). I also have long experience as an advisory and supervisory board member of businesses from the scale of start-ups to FTSE 50 sized corporates. I believe that great teams of passionate individuals with talent and diversity are at the core of the success of all enterprises and that mission and purpose driven organisations have the power to attract that talent. company_description:
We're a Commercial Expansion Platform for B2B technology companies scaling across markets — APAC MENA and Europe.

We don't advise on expansion. We execute it.

When you enter a new market you face a familiar choice: hire one senior expat and hope sign with an agency that bills hours and leaves no equity or stand up your own team and lose 12 months learning the local rules. None of these is an engine.
We build the engine. A dedicated revenue unit localized to your market: a team a playbook a network. The network gets you in the room. The team and playbook keep the pipeline coming. Three phases — Build Operate Transfer. Some clients only need the Build. Yours to take over when ready.

Two situations we see again and again:
• New Market Entry — no in-region presence we build it from scratch.
• Revenue Engine Turnaround — team in place but not delivering we diagnose fix and run it alongside your existing people until it performs.

Headquartered in Singapore. Partners in Oslo Berlin and Dubai. 35+ senior advisors and operators across the network.

As CCO at Tenix I work with operators across Europe to enable a smarter faster transition to electric fleets. Already used to manage over 6 000 vehicles across the Nordics and the Netherlands 

Our flagship solution Tenix Charge is a charger-agnostic intelligent charging platform designed to optimize energy use reduce costs and maximize uptime. Whether you operate electric buses medium-duty or heavy-duty trucks Tenix Charge ensures seamless control of your infrastructure—regardless of charger brand or fleet size.

We provide a single integrated platform for managing vehicles chargers routes and maintenance—all tailored for the realities of high-demand mission-critical operations. Let’s connect if you’re scaling your fleet or planning your charging infrastructure.
company_description:
Tenix is the specialist software company helping bus and truck operators run electric fleets that are always ready cost-efficient and future-proof.

Our Charge Operations Platform integrates charging with daily fleet management giving operators full control across depots chargers and vehicles without vendor lock-in. By combining deep operational know-how with advanced charge optimization we ensure every departure is on time energy costs are kept under control and depot power is never exceeded.

Tenix Charge guarantees fleet readiness and helps to cut charging costs with smart load balancing price optimization and automated alerts.

Tenix Fleet provides real-time visibility of vehicles drivers and maintenance to reduce downtime and improve efficiency.

Battery Health Monitoring tracks and protects battery condition over time extending lifespan and avoiding unexpected failures.

Trusted by leading transport operators across the Nordics where one in four buses runs on Tenix we bring the focus and agility of a specialist. Unlike generalist providers charging operations are our core business.

Founded in 2012 we are committed to making electrification simpler safer and more profitable for transport operators everywhere.

company_description:
Eigen Bio is a first-principles biology and AI company developing the foundational RNA model of human physiology. Our work starts from a simple premise: biology is governed by information flow and RNA is the most direct dynamic record of that flow. By decoding the transcriptome across tissues cells and circulating cfRNA we are rebuilding biological understanding from the ground up.

We combine large-scale transcriptomic data synthetic RNA generation mechanistic modeling and high-performance AI systems to create an organism-level map of how the human body changes in real time. This approach moves past symptom-driven or protein-only views of health and focuses instead on the regulatory signals that actually drive aging adaptation and disease.

Eigen Bio's mission is to turn RNA biology into a practical predictive layer of intelligence. We aim to give scientists clinicians and eventually individuals a direct window into the state and trajectory of the human organism—rooted in first principles and powered by advanced AI.

company_description:
Founded in 2010 Piton Capital is a venture capital and growth equity firm headquartered in London and investing in businesses with network effects. We make investments ranging from €200k to €20m and since inception have invested in over 50 businesses primarily in Europe.

Piton focuses exclusively on network effects businesses as this provide one of the few moats or forms of defensibility to achieve dominance. A commonly used example of this is the telephone network where the usefulness of the network increases exponentially with the number of users (or nodes) to whom you can connect. Network effects provide an "unfair advantage" that enables a winner in a respective market to achieve dominance and as a result sizeable multiples of relative market share in comparison to its competitors. Examples of this are seen in many established technology businesses such as Google Amazon (marketplace data used for recommendations search data network effects user generated reviews) Facebook Apple (iOS operating system) Microsoft (MSDOS and Windows operating systems) Alibaba (B2B marketplace) and Tencent.

Marketplaces is one form of network effects there are however several other examples of businesses that aren't marketplaces but include data network effects (including machine learning and AI) businesses that rely on user generated content as well as SaaS businesses with the potential to layer network effects around it. We evaluate and invest in businesses that exhibit the phenomenon that occurs when a product or service improves with more users.

Learn more about our portfolio companies here: https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/piton-capital

During my time as CEO of WuXiNextCODE I have overseen the integration of WuXi NextCODE's operations in the U.S. China and Iceland and led the growth of its business on four continents. WuXi NextCODE's technology underpins many of the largest and most ambitious diagnostics population genomics precision medicine and wellness endeavors worldwide.

As an entrepreneur investor and advisor I have led deals in excess of $2 Billion in the pharmaceutical industry and other fields. 

From 1997-2004 I served as Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President of Business and Finance at deCODE. During that time the company achieved worldwide recognition as the global leader in analyzing and understanding the human genome and launched the first U.S.-listed IPO by an Icelandic company. 

As a longtime advisor to deCODE genetics and its venture investors Polaris Venture Partners and ARCH Venture Partners I contributed to the strategy that culminated in the $415 million sale of deCODE genetics to Amgen in 2012. 

In the mid-2000s I served as the Chairman and CEO of Icelandair and its parent company. Prior to joining deCODE I was a consultant at McKinsey & Company serving Fortune 100 clients in the finance healthcare and hospitality sectors.

about:
Samer Hamadeh is founder & CEO of Zeel a tech-enabled healthcare company delivering in-home and in-clinic massage and tele-behavioral therapy to treat and manage physical and mental pain without opioids or surgery. Zeel's unique solution helps patients including our nation's veterans through the VA's health insurance plan access care more easily and is proven to reduce pain and stress as well as improve sleep mood and mobility. Patients report a reduction in OTC pain medication of 60% and prescription pain medication of 52%.

Samer has launched led and advised dozens of startups and tech firms since the 1990s. In 1997 Hamadeh co-founded Vault a pioneering platform for job opportunities and career information. He led the company as CEO until selling it in 2007 to a New York private-equity firm. Hamadeh entered the field of home delivery services in 2008 as an investor and board member of Dotmenu a campus food delivery service which sold to GrubHub in 2011.

Since launching Zeel in April 2013 the company has regularly ranked among the Inc. 5000 and Crain's Fast 50 as well as the Financial Times' inaugural 500 Fastest Growing Businesses in the Americas.

Hamadeh is a venture partner at Alpaca Ventures and at Lightspeed Venture Partners. In 2018 he was a finalist for the Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year award celebrating the nation's most innovative business leaders. A David Rockefeller Fellow and member of YPO Hamadeh sits on the board of the PeaceWorks Foundation and is a graduate of Stanford University.
current_position_description: Zeel allows customers and patients to seamlessly book in-home treatments through a nationwide network of more than 7 000 providers. Through massage therapy physical therapy AI-powered exercise and behavioral therapy we lift people out of mental and physical pain without the devastating side effects of prescribed and over-the-counter medications. We take special pride in serving our nation's veterans through VA Medical Centers in more than 42 states.

about:
Philip R. Hagerman co-founded Diplomat Specialty Pharmacy with his father Dale Hagerman in 1975. Diplomat grew to be the nation's largest independent specialty pharmacy with revenue growing from $25 million in 2005 to over $4 Billion in 2015 recognized as one of the fastest growing companies in healthcare and became a publicly traded company through its IPO on the NYSE in October 2014.

Mr. Hagerman led Diplomat as its CEO and Chairman of the Board of Directors for 27 years. He has a unique perspective and understanding of the business culture and history having led Diplomat through many economic cycles and operational initiatives. His day-to-day leadership of Diplomat gives him critical insights into operations strategy and competition in broad areas of healthcare and business. Throughout his career at Diplomat he has demonstrated strong entrepreneurial skills as well as regulatory marketing strategic and operational expertise. Mr. Hagerman also possesses in-depth knowledge of and key relationships in healthcare and the specialty pharmacy industry on a national basis. 

Mr. Hagerman founded SkyPoint Ventures in 2013 and remains its CEO. SkyPoint Ventures is a real estate and venture investment firm headquartered in Flint Michigan. SkyPoint Ventures focuses on early stage companies in multiple disciplines and engaging the entrepreneurial spirit while developing industry leading processes and technology. 

Mr. Hagerman currently serves as CEO of Forum Health Enterprises Inc. Forum Health founded in February 2019 focuses on building a network of integrative and functional medical practices across the nation and includes a nutraceutical division Inwell Biosciences and a technology division Power2Practice. Inwell has created a line of proprietary supplements specific to the needs of physicians in Integrative and Functional medicine. Power2Practice is an EMR and practice management software system created specifically for integrative medical practices used by over 1000 practitioners nationwide.

about:
Alexandre Lazarow is a global venture capitalist and the Managing Partner of Fluent Ventures. 

He has invested in 7 unicorns. His portfolio includes Chime Kin Neon Sidecar Health Facily FinAccel ZenBusiness Kueski Clara Xepelin Kamino Sabi Brkz Flychain Terminal among others.

Alex is the author of Out-Innovate: How Global Entrepreneurs - from Delhi to Detroit - Are Rewriting the Rules of Silicon Valley (Harvard Business Review Press). Out-Innovate has been translated to Korean Chinese and Spanish so far. Alex is a Senior Contributor with Forbes a columnist with Entrepreneur Magazine and author of the newsletter [99%Tech].

about:
Venkat Srinivasan is a serial entrepreneur and investor. Innospark Ventures is an evergreen venture fund investing in early stage AI led startups. He brings deep expertise in AI and computational algorithms to the firm especially natural language understanding combined with deep functional knowledge in multiple domains including operations research finance and accounting. He has founded several successful AI-led startups including eCredit Corporate Fundamentals and Rage Frameworks. More recently he has founded EnglishHelper (using AI to solve illiteracy at scale) Gyan.AI (first explainable natural language understanding engine) Creda Health (Digital Health Assistant for continuum of care) and PrismX (automated software development platform). 

Venkat was honored with a Lifetime Achievement Award by The Indus Entrepreneurs [TiE] in 2017 (http://bit.ly/2j5ElGG). Dr. Srinivasan holds 9 patents has published over 30 research papers in prestigious peer-reviewed journals has co-edited two books and is the author of a book titled The Intelligent Enterprise in the Era of Big Data (Wiley 2017). Venkat has strongly advocated for and believes that successful AI-led transformation will be characterized by explainability traceability context awareness and the ability to learn from sparse data environments.

about:
A 2017 Boston Business Journal 40 Under 40 Honoree Fady is the Founder and General Partner of Cybernetix Ventures a venture capital firm leading the way for investment into early-stage robotics automation and AI startups. He is also Co-founder of MassRobotics the first and largest robotics & AI startup escalator in the world. He led the multi-million dollar fundraising for the organization and architected MassRobotics' corporate partnership program and business model. Fady is the first to coin the term "startup escalator" to differentiate the unique model that he developed at MassRobotics to help startups bridge the chasm from prototyping to production. While at MassRobotics Fady forged more than 40 strategic corporate partnership with companies like Amazon Robotics FedEx Honda Mitsubishi Analog Devices Autodesk among others. He pioneered innovative corporate-startup programs that included the Mitsubishi Startup Engagement Program The Festo Healthcare Robotics Catalyst Program AWS Accelerator Program and the Autodesk Robotics in Construction Annual Event

Early in his career Fady was a Regional Manager of Nokia Siemens Networks in North Africa and Europe where he led and managed multimillion-euro acquisitions and bids. He was also part of the team who led the merger between Siemens Com and Nokia Networks in North Africa for 700+ employees. 

Fady has been a frequent speaker advisor judge panelist and mentor in multiple entrepreneurship organizations and events and authored numerous articles on robotics investing and innovation. Fady has an educational background in complex systems management mechanical engineering and design.
company_description: Cybernetix Ventures is a venture capital firm leading the way for investment into early-stage robotics automation and AI startups. about:
Kathryn Wortsman is Managing Partner of Amplify Capital one Canada's oldest and leading impact funds. Amplify Capital focuses on investing in businesses using technology to solve for massive social and environmental challenges. Kathryn began her career as an investment banker at RBC Dominion Securities and then went on to spend 20+ years in private equity and venture capital both in Toronto and New York City. Kathryn has held senior investment roles at Constellation Ventures (a Bear Stearns GP Fund) MetLife Ventures BMO Capital and Lynx Equity. Kathryn started her philanthropy work in New York and then launched the Toronto Chapter of Social Venture Partners in 2008. Kathryn launched the first fund of Amplify MaRS Catalyst Fund in 2016 with backing from Richard Branson's family foundation Virgin Unite Lino et Mirella Saputo Foundation McConnell Foundation and a number of family offices in Canada.

Prior to Amplify Kathryn co-founded Crest Capital in 2012 a special purpose acquisition vehicle which purchased 100% of Kelso Industrial Group in 2013. Kathryn and her business partner managed and grew Kelso through 2015 when Kathryn exited Kelso to move on to MaRS/Amplify. 

Kathryn has over 20 years of private equity and venture capital investment experience in Canada and the US. Prior to joining Lynx which she led the growth of the portfolio by over 10x Kathryn was a Director at BMO Capital Corporation investing mezzanine capital to small mid market companies. Prior to BMOCC Kathryn held consecutive investment roles at MetLife in New York: Principal in the venture group and Director Corporate Development. Prior to MetLife Kathryn was VP and Principal at Constellation Ventures in New York a Bear Sterns sponsored venture capital firm focused on early and growth stage media enabling technologies. Prior to moving to New York Kathryn held positions at both RBC Dominion Securities and CIBC Capital Partners in Toronto. 

Kathryn holds a BA in Economics from the University of Western Ontario and an MBA from Columbia University. Kathryn was the Founder and first Chair of the Board of Social Venture Partners Toronto established in 2008. SVP Toronto is part of the international network focused on active and engaged philanthropy. 

Specialties: Impact Investing Impact Measurement Board Governance and Board management Fundraising Investor relations Deal negotiation pricing and completion. Financial and Impact reporting and operational capacity building.

about:
It all started with brownies and baseball cards. Then came lemonade and a larger card table. Before long my 8-year old sidewalk empire had a formal pricing scheme two part-time employees (my parents) and even a few not-so-hostile acquisitions. 

Fourteen years later after a surprisingly successful Beanie Babies business and Pokémon coding company in middle school I sold my second (real) business. In many ways Puppystock (2005-2007) was the natural extension of my neighborhood dessert and card dealing business. While my co-founder and I eventually grew our tiny little online store into a multi-million dollar company and disrupted how pet products were sold we were soon distracted by the shine of a new idea. Before we sold Puppystock we had already started on FriendTones (2007-2008) – a mobile social networking and technology company intertwined with the new Facebook application market. Although one of our applications became the Napster of ringtones (when people actually used ringtones) I still use pieces of our technology every day with my phone and so do you. 

By 2008 ringtones had lost their popularity and I had moved on to designing creating and managing three analytics programs for Director-level personnel at two Intelligence agencies (CIA & NGA). From 2011-2013 I acquired experience at a family investment office and worked on the full lifecycle of multiple US-based venture capital deals with a prominent angel investor. From 2013-2014 I dually completed a full-time MBA program and co-founded a new family office that managed alternative investment funds for a very wealthy family abroad (and became an international tax expert).

Now I’ve found my next lemonade stand – with the opportunity to leverage my experiences as a serial entrepreneur (former) tornado chaser turned venture capitalist at Early Light Ventures.

company_description:
Medical transport should be a critical part of the patient journey not a component that creates additional clinical or financial risk. Yet for too long this segment of healthcare has lacked structure and oversight leaving patients hospitals and health plans paying the price.

Alacura offers a comprehensive solution coordinating every aspect of a successful medical transport from bedside to bedside.

Our Rapid Transport Response platform is a clinical triage and logistics solution that connects hospitals patients health plans and medical transporters 24/7. It ensures the right mode of transport is provided at the right time lowering the risk of clinical complications and unnecessary expense. 

With a team that's 50% physicians and flight nurses we understand the clinical needs of high-acuity patients the administrative challenges transports pose to hospitals and the compliance concerns of health plans. By managing these details from start to finish we give all parties the clarity and confidence they need throughout the transportation process.

Our national network of credentialed transport partners enables us to coordinate the appropriate mode of transportation for every case whether it requires ground rotor or fixed-wing.

For patients our solution provides safety peace of mind and no surprise bills. For hospitals they have a trusted partner and no additional administrative burden. And for health plans Alacura means happier members strict compliance with the No Surprises Act and no hidden liabilities.

Since 2015 Alacura has brought safety efficiency and order to a part of healthcare that has gone without them for too long.

Learn more at alacura.com and follow us on LinkedIn for additional insights on how we deliver coordination and clarity to medical transportation.

about:
Michelle Kennedy is the founder and CEO of Peanut one of the fastest-growing social networking apps for women. Listed as Apple's Trend of the Year 2021 TIME100's Most Influential Companies 2022 and Fast Company's Most Innovative 2023 Peanut has become a coveted destination for women looking to find friendship and support throughout all stages of motherhood.

The idea was born when Michelle was expecting her first child in 2014. She realised that there weren't any social networks for women to connect across key life stages. As the former deputy CEO of dating behemoth Badoo and an inaugural board member of Bumble Kennedy understood how powerful online communities were. And so true to the entrepreneurial core of founders she decided to solve her need by creating a new kind of social network where women could connect chat and meet.

Having worked in social discovery for ten years Michelle has unprecedented experience and understanding of the safety and growth components of building a social network for women and continues to execute on her mission to make Peanut the leading network to connect women when they need each other most. 

Michelle also runs her own fund StartHER where she is committed to challenging the biases in venture capital and giving everyone a fair chance by re-shaping funding dynamics providing access to professional networks and ultimately becoming home to the new faces of entrepreneurship. Michelle's investments include Linktree Sunroom Intimately Lex and more.

 Reach me at michellekennedy.co

about:
Trina Spear is the Co-CEO and Co-Founder of FIGS the digitally native DTC healthcare apparel and lifestyle brand creating technically advanced products for the modern healthcare professional. FIGS is on a mission to celebrate empower and serve healthcare professionals our AWESOME HUMANS.

FIGS has revolutionized the large and fragmented healthcare apparel industry by branding a previously unbranded industry and de-commoditizing a previously commoditized product. Under Trina’s leadership FIGS has created premium products for healthcare professionals introduced a convenient and seamless DTC online platform and built a community around a profession.

Trina’s accolades include being named in Goldman Sachs’ 100 Most Intriguing Entrepreneurs (2018 & 2019) Endeavor Entrepreneur (Endeavor 2015) Entrepreneur of the Year (Ernst & Young Los Angeles Region 2018). Her article “Transforming the Healthcare Experience: Doctors Nurses Patients and Beyond ” was published in the Journal of the American College of Radiology in 2017. Additionally under Trina’s leadership FIGS was ranked Number 21 on Inc. Magazine’s Inc. 5000 ranking of the nation’s fastest-growing companies in 2018.

Prior to launching FIGS Trina was an associate with the Blackstone Group – a leading global alternative investment business – in the firm’s hedge fund group. She also spent four years with Citigroup's investment banking and private equity divisions. Trina is a member of the 2018 Class of Henry Crown Fellows at the Aspen Institute. Trina received her BA in Economics from Tufts University graduating magna cum laude and she completed her MBA at Harvard Business School.

company_description:
At Behind Genius Ventures we believe in the business value of storytelling. We prioritize founders who demonstrate strong storytelling capabilities. We take a people-oriented approach to investing and the evaluation of founders. We maintain a proudly generalist investing thesis in order to stay flexible and adapt as the market changes allowing us to back the best and brightest founders. As a firm with a solo GP we want to feel strong conviction in the founder's problem-solution. We value autonomy in our decision-making process and hope our investment becomes a signal to other investors. If there's one X factor the companies we back share in common it's that design and beauty are valued alongside functional products.

Paige Finn Doherty is the Managing Partner of Behind Genius Ventures an early-stage venture capital firm that leans into the power of storytelling to back timeless companies building the future of work and play. company_description:
Founded in 2010 Piton Capital is a venture capital and growth equity firm headquartered in London and investing in businesses with network effects. We make investments ranging from €200k to €20m and since inception have invested in over 50 businesses primarily in Europe.

Piton focuses exclusively on network effects businesses as this provide one of the few moats or forms of defensibility to achieve dominance. A commonly used example of this is the telephone network where the usefulness of the network increases exponentially with the number of users (or nodes) to whom you can connect. Network effects provide an "unfair advantage" that enables a winner in a respective market to achieve dominance and as a result sizeable multiples of relative market share in comparison to its competitors. Examples of this are seen in many established technology businesses such as Google Amazon (marketplace data used for recommendations search data network effects user generated reviews) Facebook Apple (iOS operating system) Microsoft (MSDOS and Windows operating systems) Alibaba (B2B marketplace) and Tencent.

Marketplaces is one form of network effects there are however several other examples of businesses that aren't marketplaces but include data network effects (including machine learning and AI) businesses that rely on user generated content as well as SaaS businesses with the potential to layer network effects around it. We evaluate and invest in businesses that exhibit the phenomenon that occurs when a product or service improves with more users.

Learn more about our portfolio companies here: https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/piton-capital

I work at the intersection of proven energy transition technologies and the corporate and policy realities of deploying them across Asia Pacific. At Startupbootcamp I've connected APAC corporates with technology companies — moving from technology screening to commercial deployment with a focus on what the project economics actually look like.

Right now I'm mapping a specific gap: why carbon capture technology that is proven and commercially deployed in North America and Europe hasn't moved at scale in Asia Pacific — and what the project finance policy and offtake structures would need to look like for that to change.

My mission is to rebuild the American dream through entrepreneurship.

***Quick guide to my content & connecting***
 I invest through https://springtimeventures.com/ 
 Fundraising advice http://vcminute.co

***How to tell me about your startup***
 Open Office Hours http://richm.us/oh
 Read about SpringTime and share your deck http://richm.us/stvlearn
 Not through a cold LinkedIn Invite 

***My Personal Vision***
I believe that technology has brought us both great opportunity and great peril but that technology is indifferent to its own results. It is up to humankind to use technology for the good of all. I believe that on an individual basis humans are good and strive for the same opportunities: to grow learn and earn. 

I respect all people who drive to start up: people who build something out of nothing create financial independence and solve problems. I believe this drive is universal and all people everywhere deserve the right to start.

When I look at the division anger and vitriol in the world at large I also see the hope growth and community in the startup world. I see the adaptability grit passion and striving of entrepreneurs everywhere. I’m inspired by it every day. 

I believe that entrepreneurship is the highest ideal of the American dream.

I believe that entrepreneurship will save America from a downward spiral of inequity. I believe it will rebuild the middle class. I believe technology empowers people—regular people like you and me—to once again directly influence the government. And I believe that through innovation startups are going to transform the world giving all people the opportunity to grow learn and earn.

company_description:
The Challenge:
67% of American women wear sizes 14+—that's 100 million women. Yet this majority is systematically underserved by the retail industry. The plus size community faces paltry options unwelcoming environments and a lack of representation—all adding up to a disappointing experience with style.

Our Vision:
Style freedom through a life well-lived

The Dia & Co Solution:
Dia & Co is the only retail service dedicated to meeting the plus size community's full range of style needs. We're applying the best of data and technology to serve women who have been ignored for far too long—from developing the shopping experiences she's always wanted to building community platforms that inspire her to ensuring she sees herself represented in the world around her.

Our Approach — Representative Retail:
Dia & Co is a company built around a customer not a business model. Our community leads the way and we meet their unique needs by creating products and experiences across three comprehensive areas of service:

Commerce:
Curated access to the best-fitting Style and Active apparel (led
by Dia & Co's beloved owned brands) fully equipping her to express herself through style. Customized shopping experiences to remove the stress around fit for a joyful and fabulous experience.

Community:
Platforms to foster deep connections between members both of offline and online offering a place for her to be celebrated and to celebrate others through style.

Change:
Campaigns to create lasting change by combating stereotypes and biases. Advocacy to increase representation of our community outside the Dia ecosystem so she can see herself reflected in style.
headline: Co-Founder at Dia&Co

about:
I rarely check LinkedIn messages. If you'd like to reach out please email me at leo@humbaventures.com.

I'm a co-founder of Susa Ventures (generalist seed fund) and its sister fund Humba Ventures (deep tech seed fund). I spend most of my time on Humba where I invest $250k-$1m into companies building in deep tech and in critical sectors (manufacturing energy defense etc).

Prior to Susa and Humba I was the second engineering hire at Linkedin where I helped build the first versions of products like LinkedIn Jobs and LinkedIn Groups. I then worked on payment fraud detection algorithms at Google and was also an early engineer at Factual where I built data cleaning processing and deduping software.

Blog: https://www.codingvc.com/ 
Twitter: https://twitter.com/lpolovets

about:
David joined Greylock Partners in January 2000. He focuses on Consumer Internet Social Networking Media Convergence Marketplaces and Games. 

David was ranked #16 on the 2014 Forbes Midas List #10 on the 2013 Forbes Midas List #4 on the 2012 Forbes Midas List and #9 on the 2011 Forbes Midas List.

David's portfolio companies have included: Roblox (NYSE: RBLX) Facebook (NASDAQ: FB) LinkedIn (NYSE: LNKD subsequently acquired by Microsoft) and Pandora (NYSE: P subsequently acquired by SiriusXM).

He was also on the boards of Vessel (acquired by Verizon) VUDU (acquired by Walmart and subsequently NBCUniversal) PullString (acquired by Apple) WhoSay (acquired by Viacom) Revision3 (acquired by Discovery Communications) Oodle (acquired by QVC) SGN (acquired by MindJolt) and New Edge Networks (acquired by EarthLink). 

He presently sits on the boards of Nextdoor (NYSE: KIND) Discord and JubileeTV. David is a member of the Advisory Board of Northern Light a leading early-stage VC firm in China. 

David is also a Trustee of Yale University and Rockefeller University a world-renowned center for research and graduate education in the biomedical sciences chemistry bioinformatics and physics. 

He serves on the Advisory Boards of MLT.org Questbridge The McLaren Racing Group and is a founding investor in BrainMind.org.

Before coming to Greylock David spent over seven years in product management and development at consumer internet gaming and media companies ranging in size from small startups through publically traded companies. David was employee number 30 and the first consumer product marketing hire at Excite.com in early 1996. He held roles as GM of Excite.com and VP of Content and Programming for the Excite Network and eventually became SVP of Product Strategy at Excite and then Excite@Home until early 2000 when he joined Greylock. Before Excite David was in product marketing at Electronic Arts and in product development at Crystal Dynamics a gaming start-up later acquired by Eidos Interactive. He started his career in management consulting with Marakon Associates and The Boston Consulting Group and also spent time at HBO.

David holds a BA from Yale University and an MBA from Stanford University

company_description: SignalFire is the first venture capital firm built like a technology company to better solve for the needs of founders. The core of its value-add is Beacon the AI engine SignalFire has been refining since the firm's launch in 2013. Beacon tracks more than 600 million employees and 80 million companies to guide the fund's investing and assist portfolio companies with scaling their teams and revenue. SignalFire also helps early-stage founders navigate the toughest parts of building a company at every stage with expert advisors 100 skill-building workshops a year and an in-house team of recruiters data scientists PR experts and go-to-market leaders. With over $2.1 billion in assets under management SignalFire focuses on investing from seed to scale. The firm's key sectors include AI/ML developer tools B2B SaaS healthcare cybersecurity and consumer. https://www.signalfire.com/disclosures
